input.initialPrompt:
I have an ObservableCollection<Product> with Name and Price properties.
Show me how to display these products in a vertical scrollable list using
CollectionView with a DataTemplate that shows both the name and price.
Use compiled bindings.

data.content:
Here's a concise example:

**Product.cs**
```csharp
public class Product
{
    public string Name { get; set; }
    public decimal Price { get; set; }
}
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ProductsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    public ObservableCollection<Product> Products { get; } = new()
    {
        new Product { Name = "Widget", Price = 9.99m },
        new Product { Name = "Gadget", Price = 24.95m }
    };
}
```

**XAML** — note `x:DataType` on the page and inside the `DataTemplate` for compiled bindings:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:models="clr-namespace:YourApp.Models"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             x:DataType="vm:ProductsViewModel"
             x:Class="YourApp.ProductsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Products}"
                    ItemsLayout="VerticalList">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Product">
                <HorizontalStackLayout Padding="10" Spacing="12">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                           FontSize="16"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}"
                           FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </HorizontalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

**Key points:**

- **`x:DataType`** on the page targets the ViewModel; inside the `DataTemplate` it targets `Product`. This enables compiled bindings — caught at build time, faster at runtime.
- **`ItemsLayout="VerticalList"`** is the default, so it's optional but explicit.
- `CollectionView` virtualizes items and scrolls vertically out of the box — no `ScrollView` wrapper needed.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) code example showing how to use CollectionView with compiled bindings to display a list of products.
